caustica.options.rt.exposureMode.tooltip: How the ray-traced image is mapped to display brightness. Auto adapts to scene luminance; Manual uses the exposure value directly.
caustica.options.rt.manualEv.tooltip: Exposure compensation in stops. In Manual, this is the fixed exposure. In Auto, this biases the auto exposure brighter or darker.
caustica.options.rt.spp.tooltip: Path-tracing samples taken per pixel each frame. Higher is cleaner but more demanding.
caustica.options.rt.maxBounces.tooltip: Maximum number of secondary path-tracing bounces after the primary hit. Higher captures more indirect light but costs more.
caustica.options.rt.sunSize.tooltip: Angular radius of the sun and moon disk. Larger discs cast softer shadow penumbrae.
caustica.options.rt.cloudSettings.toolti: Configure the ray-marched volumetric cloud layer.
caustica.options.rt.cloudSettings.title: Volumetric Cloud Settings
caustica.rt.volumetricClouds.tooltip: Render ray-marched volumetric clouds in Overworld-style skies.
caustica.rt.cloudHeight.tooltip: Center height between the upper and lower boundaries of the volumetric cloud layer.
caustica.rt.cloudThickness.tooltip: Vertical thickness of the actual volumetric cloud layer. Higher values create deeper clouds that retain visible volume when viewed from within the layer.
caustica.rt.cloudCoverage.tooltip: Fraction of the sky covered by volumetric clouds. Higher values create broader cloud fields.
caustica.rt.cloudDensity.tooltip: Optical density of volumetric clouds. 100% is the default; higher values make clouds more opaque and darker.
caustica.rt.cloudShape.tooltip: Strength of the large-scale cumulus body and tower shaping. 100% is the default.
caustica.rt.cloudBottomVariation.tooltip: Variation and carving along cloud undersides. 0% produces calmer bases; 200% produces more irregular bases.
caustica.rt.cloudDetailStrength.tooltip: Strength of medium-scale billows and density detail. 100% is the default.
caustica.rt.cloudEdgeErosion.tooltip: Strength of fine turbulent erosion along cloud boundaries. 0% disables fine erosion; 200% doubles it.
caustica.options.rt.entities.tooltip: Include entities and block entities in the ray-traced scene.
caustica.options.rt.particles.tooltip: Draw particles as camera-ray billboards inside the traced scene.
caustica.options.rt.glow.tooltip: Draw the vanilla Glowing-effect outline (through walls) around glowing entities.
caustica.options.rt.waterWaves.tooltip: Animate water-surface normals for moving wave highlights.
caustica.options.rt.fog.tooltip: Enable ray-marched 3D volumetric fog with exponential height falloff in the ray-traced world.
caustica.options.rt.fogDensity.tooltip: Extinction density at the base height. Higher values produce thicker fog and reduce visibility.
caustica.options.rt.fogAmbientDensityFlo: Minimum fog density retained at high altitude, as a percentage of the density at the base height. This prevents the exponential height envelope from fading to zero.
caustica.options.rt.fogAmbientLightCoupl: How much ambient fog participates in directional scattering and sun/moon light-path extinction. At 0%, directional scattering uses only height fog.
caustica.options.rt.fogNoiseStrength.too: Amount of 3D world-space density variation. 0% is smooth fog; higher values create more distinct pockets and wisps while preserving the average density.
caustica.options.rt.fogNoiseScale.toolti: Approximate world-space size of the 3D density features. Larger values create broader, slower-changing fog structures.
caustica.options.rt.fogHeightFalloff.too: How quickly fog density decreases toward the ambient fog floor with altitude. Higher values keep the denser layer closer to the base height.
caustica.options.rt.fogVerticalOpticalDe: Directional artistic control that increases optical thickness for steep downward and upward views through the fog layer; horizontal sightlines remain at 1.0x.
caustica.options.rt.fogBaseHeight.toolti: World height where the configured fog density applies.
caustica.options.rt.atmosphericScatterin: Enable directional Rayleigh and Mie scattering from the sun and moon through the volumetric fog.
caustica.options.rt.scatteringStrength.t: Scale the sun and moon's directional Rayleigh and Mie scattering contribution.
caustica.options.rt.hdr.tooltip: Present in HDR (ST.2084/PQ) instead of SDR, when the display supports it. Takes effect after restarting the game.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPaperWhite.toolti: Absolute brightness (in nits) that SDR-equivalent scene brightness maps to on an HDR display.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPeak.tooltip: Absolute brightness (in nits) highlights roll off toward. Set to your display's peak HDR brightness.
caustica.options.rt.dlssQuality.tooltip: DLSS Ray Reconstruction quality mode. Lower-quality modes render fewer pixels and upscale more aggressively for higher framerates; higher-quality modes render more pixels for a sharper image. Only affects the image when DLSS Ray Reconstruction is enabled.
caustica.options.rt.debugView.tooltip: Visualize an intermediate ray-tracing buffer instead of the final image.
